People with worldly, carnal minds take pride in their possessions, forgetting that whatever we have, we received it from God, and should use it for God. Why, then, do we boast? Self is the great idol which all the world worships, to the detriment of God and his sovereignty. God can force men out of what they consider most secure and comfortable. Such a one, and all who side with him, will perish together. Thus end people's pride, presumption, and carnal security. The Lord is against those who harm his people, and even more against those who lead them into sin. Egypt will be a kingdom again, but it will be the lowest of the kingdoms; it will have little wealth and power. History shows the complete fulfillment of this prophecy. God, not only in justice, but in wisdom and goodness to us, breaks the supports on which we rely, so that they are no longer our confidence.

The attackers of Tyre obtained little plunder. But when God uses ambitious or covetous men, he will reward them according to their desires; for every person shall have his reward. God had mercy in store for the house of Israel soon after. The history of nations best explains ancient prophecies. All events fulfill the Scriptures. Thus, even in the most difficult times, the Lord sows the seed of our future prosperity. Happy are those who desire his favor, grace, and image; they will delight in his service, and not covet any earthly reward; and the blessings they have chosen will surely be theirs forever.
